div1.entryFree: Blench, vb., to start: if he but b., I know my course, Hml. II, 2, 626. patience herself doth lesser b. at sufferance than I do, Troil. I, 1, 28 (v. less and lesser). Hence == to fly off, to be inconstant: hold you ever to our special drift, though sometimes you do b. from this to that, Meas. IV, 5, 5. could man so b.? Wint. I, 2, 333. there can be no evasion to b. from this and to stand firm by honour, Troil. II, 2, 68.
